reason: Enabling StrictModes is a standard SSH hardening practice that prevents logins if user configuration files are insecurely permissioned. Since the default is often already enabled or the change is a simple configuration addition, the risk of system breakage is extremely low, though users with incorrectly permissioned .ssh directories may experience login failures.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Users with world-writable .ssh directories or home directories may be unable to log in via SSH after remediation
